name: Antinov
description:
[center][img=989254]AntinovName[/img][/center]
[center][img=989246]Antinov[/img][heading][color=white][u]The Amorous Avian Aristocrat[/u][/color][/heading][/center][hr]
[b]A[/b]ntinov is a princely (or sometimes [i]princess[/i]-ly) bird of fluid gender, as most of his species are. In particular Antinov is a suave, seductive and often romantic individual. That smooth flirtatious outer layer hides a cunning, manipulative, calculating and intelligent mindset that most overlook in the face of amorous advances from the alien avian. But alongside that cunning mindset Anti is loyal to his friends, nation and allies. Antinov is quite masterful at compartmentalizing though, not letting work or personal life clash most of the time so neither is truely compromised.

In truth Anti is neither entirely male, nor female as his species is sexually monomorphic and swaps back and forth depending on emotional and environmental factors as a more true-to-reality hermaphrodite. Nothing entirely disappears, but there are some subtle differences depending on active hormones, subtle extra curves, less subtle engorged breasts during a female phase. And more noticeably engorged male bits during a male phase. Anti generally uses male pronouns for himself, but is indifferent to which others choose. (Often playing into the gender role others seem to desire.) Anti only develops noticeable breasts if something triggers him to lactate, but when Anti does develop a bust, it is fairly sizable between milkings. (Do feel free to imagine or treat anti as male or female. Just no herm pronouns, please.)

When not on his princely duties, Antinov can often be found frequenting the [color=purple][user]Paradise Club[/user][/color], a somewhat illicit club catering to the prince's darker desires, and letting him sing and dance to express himself and relax. ne can foten find the big blue singing away on stage. You can easily find your way there by visiting [u]Asteroid EE 151[/u] in the Epsilon Eridani system.

[collapse=💎 Personality:]
[b]A[/b]ntinov is overall a rather charming and amorous individual, with a very broad range of interests and a forward nature. Like most of his kind Anti doesn't have a polar gender identity and wanders between masculine and feminine depending on how his (or [i]her[/i]) hormone levels currently are. Antinov is generally very relaxed overall, laid back, but still prim and proper when he needs to be. This calm and cool nature tends to persist, even under pressure and direct threat. This can tend to be unnerving to some. Though at times Antinov can get quite exited, when enjoying food, or the company of a lover, or even in the heat of fighting. In particular Anti has an exited reaction to feeling the life of something slip away in his grasp.

Antinov is a rather charismatic individual, and quite outgoing when he needs to be, often times being quite a flirty birdie. Though at times he can be quite reserved as well, keeping to himself and being quiet. When on the job Antinov is very professional and reserved, but when relaxing or having fun he can be pretty wild and almost feral at times. Anti is especially wild and untamed when intoxicated on the pheromones of a lover, generally the more he loves the individual, the more wild the reaction. Antinov has a taste for cooking and is quite good at it, many times he's found in the kitchen aboard his ship helping out with meals for the crew.

Generally, Anti is quite caring and loving to those he likes. His subordinates, lovers and family take first priority for him. Though he also has a strong camaraderie for all his countrymen too. A defining feature of Antinov is how far he will go to protect those he cares for, and to settle any debt that may be incurred against him or loved ones. Antinov can be downright murderously vengeful when he can get away with it, but Antinov knows where to stop to avoid turning a vendetta into a source of more strife for himself and loved ones. In some rare occasions, Anti's loving nature can turn predatory, pushing him to gobble up loved ones greedily and sometimes fatally.

Out of uniform Antinov primarily wears effeminate clothing, dresses, stockings, corsets, showing off his curves and male attributes equally, it's easy to see he's proud of his effeminacy and masculinity equally. Like much of his species, Antinov has an attraction to shiny objects, gold, silver, platinum, even smooth shiny scales and glittering jewels. Antinov feels a compulsion to take and hoard such treasures, primarily within his crop. When his horde gets too full, he generally lets it down into his guts, processing it all. Of course this usually prompts Antinov to try and restock his horde, and he's not above hording shiny creatures away inside either.

[/collapse]
[collapse=💎 Extended Bio:]
[b]A[/b]ntinov's history is a long and convoluted past, starting from a troubled childhood which promptly lead into military service in a galaxy spanning Empire. As an adult Antinov has become a somewhat polar personality, which can switch when needed, or during duress. His story begins on a watery world called Zeiophine, where the Avalonian was born to [user]Kacylia[/user] and grew up there, far from his avian-esque kin back on Avalon. This childhood made him quite close to the fishy natives of Zeiophine, sharing an odd connection with them and making a number of childhood friends who would pla a part in his later life. When he was only 15 ISY, Anti was identified as having great potential for mind machine interface usage. He was moved from standard schooling to a special division of the local arm of the Imperial Military academy for further study.

After a year under strict observation, the young Avalonian was assessed as an excellent candidate for mind machine interface. And with some protest from his mother, he was moved to specialized training known as “Psionic Opperations” (or simply "Psi-Ops:) along with his identical sister [user]Milena[/user]. His time in the Psi ops division was anything but pleasant, after a long and dark run, it had become borderline corrupt and abused the siblings. Antinov was often split up from his sister, and within a few months stopped seeing her entirely as his program and hers diverged. Antinov soon after met up with a young Sergal by the name of Kurai, only a few years younger than him, in time, he started to be like a big brother to her.

Antinov and Kurai formed a strong bond and began to stick closely together during training. Together the two really began to blossom. The training was tough, but Antinov and Kurai stuck it out and met every challenge, that is until other abilities started to manifest in Kurai over the years. As it turned out, Antinov's adopted little sister had immense potential for controlling machines and other systems with her brainwaves and was quite receptive to information transmitted back without the need for disruptive implants. Not unlike his blood sister, Kurai would too be stripped away and taken for specialized training. This affected Anti quite negatively, so some leeway was made to let him visit with Kurai and Milena.

Feeling isolated from the two, Antinov decided to undergo augmentation to bring him up to their level, so he could once again be at their sides and not limited to short intermittent meetings. But the process was a harsh one, and caused much suffering for the young Avalonian. With Antinov suffering so, Kurai and Milena concocted a plan to escape Psi Ops and expose the growing corruption within the division. One night, the two escaped their rooms and freed Antinov. Together the three made their way to the administrative officer’s quarters and broke in. While Milena worked on breaking into the main computer terminal, Kurai and Anti kept watch.

The administrative officer did end up visiting his office despite the late time, but the Sergal and Avalonian jumped him and dragged him inside quietly. From there a forceful interrogation followed, gleaning the three the access credentials to all files stored on site and remotely. From there, the three forwarded every bit of evidence to Imperial high command. Being that Antinov’s mother was on the High Council, she was quick to pursue the information to protect her children. This lead to a swift shut down of the Psi Ops division.

All three found their night of subterfuge and sneaking quite appealing, so they arranged to be transferred to covert ops to train for a position within the Imperial intelligence Division. In covert ops, the three new bonded as siblings, settled in well and started to become increasingly closer to one another. Eventually the three would graduate, and move on to placement within a tactical unit in the IID. Over the following years the three would accomplish quite a lot, mostly in regards to dealing crippling blows to piracy organizations and other more civil matters as the Empire was in a long period of peacetime.

Antinov soon became more than just a sister to Kurai, the two falling in love as time went on. Although there was some jealousy from Milena at this, eventually the three fell into a deeper, even incestuous relationship. It would all culminate in the three connecting themselves to the same mind machine interface and letting their minds touch, thoughts intermingle. This created a deep intricate bond between the three, bringing them even closer than they could have ever imagined previously. And since then, while in close proximity, the three can communicate without speaking and share thoughts via the same systems that let them speak to the non-invasive mind machine interface. This link only worked over distances within arm’s length for the mostpart, however.

Antinov's troubles would not end with his departure from psi ops though, while he excelled at every part of his training and missions in covert ops, something else loomed on the horizon. Antinov's performance was so impressive that both he and Kurai were selected to become deep cover operatives and be dropped on an enemy world to gather intel, while Milena would serve as their backup. Antinov relished it at first, the challenge, the thrill, and the ability to exercise his growing silver tongue pleased the now adolescent Avalonian. However, fate would deal him another rough blow.

With their first deployment, came Antinov's realization, he had a very sensitive stomach. The local cuisine, foreign to him in every way, deeply upset his guts. At first it wasn't too bad, a bit of gas here, a stomach-ache there, but as the days ticked on and he kept eating the unfamiliar foods his belly kept getting more and more upset. Eventually it grew to the point where Anti was rendered unable to perform his duties, unable to even leave his bed other than to drag himself to the bathroom. At that point, Anti had to be withdrawn, as a hospital visit might compromise his cover.
 
When high command was made privy to Antinov's condition, and how crippling it was for an infiltrator, he was discharged and stripped of rank. The combination of loosing not only what he'd worked so hard for, but being split up from Kurai and Milena as they were kept in black ops drove Antinov to near insanity. Antinov had loved what he was doing, and being close to his sisters. The Avalonian would eventually recover his faculties and take up a more freelance life, working as a mercenary of sorts. Anti used his mercenary work to spy on many factions and enemies of the Empire, feeding intel to the Empire through back channels. Antinov had an end goal, however, he amassed favors and fortune until he could enhance his body further and remove all weaknesses from it. Then Anti could once again be with his siblings, with Milena and Kurai.

[hr]
Later down the road Antinov would find himself caught up in the first TSU conflict, a guerrilla war between the Empire and another faction known as the Trans Stellar Union, which was made up of races the Empire had either tried to exterminate, or who heard of the Empire's sheer brutality and were turned against them. Antinov managed to meet up with Kurai during this conflict and the two reconnected, resulting in Antinov joining the squadron Kurai and his sister Milena were in. The two fought side by side in the various skirmishes and battles of the first TSU conflict as soldiers in the 1st Autonomous corps, up until an incident that would rock their autonomous black ops unit.

Following an incident where one of the more unstable members of his newfound squadron going rogue and killing many of their comrades, their unit was dissolved and Antinov took it harshly, vanishing from the Imperial military altogether and becoming a mercenary once more. Antinov was back to fighting as a one Avalonian black ops unit, supporting the Empire in his own ways as well as perfecting his skills against and with other mercs. Again circumstance had torn him away from his loved ones and tarnished his name. But after quite some time, Kurai would again be discovered by Kurai, who had quite an offer for the wayward prince.

Kurai had risen to the rank of captain, and ran her own ship. In the decades Anti had been away, the autonomous fleets resurfaced. And there was a position open for him within a small battle group known as the 24th autonomous. In his time apart from Kurai, Milena had chosen to go her own way too, but what’s more, Kurai had been reunited with her flesh and blood brother who had been in command of the 24’th. But tragically, he had been killed in action, sacrificing himself for Kurai. The Sergal wanted Antinov to join her as her second in command and rebuild the 24th. Of course Antinov accepted, and helped rebuild the unit, eventually bringing his sister into the fold as well to reunite his family.

Through effort and hardship, Antinov built himself into a respectable officer not just within the 24’th, but through the entire Imperial Armada, clearing any past shame and doubt from his name. It was here at his pinnacle that Antinov reconnected with his mother, after so many years apart. Now in his Prime, Anti commanded a logistical frigate under the name Resolute, holding much of the 24th’s ground forces. This ship served as home to him, and his mother during her lengthy visits. While Kurai and his sister Milena operated out of a camouflage freighter that made up the main part of the spaceborne part of the 24th. With time, the newly reformed 24th, would come to be known as Azure. A playful nod to most of the ranking members having an Azure colouration, so it became the official trim colour of the duty uniforms, BDU’s and combat armour.

In his position with Kurai, Antinov is afforded total autonomy as he had when working as a rogue. In a way, given the link him and Kurai share he has an equal input on all matters undertaken by the squadron. They very easily reach consensus on what to do, even when separate, due to the years they spent mentally connected. This makes Antinov the perfect spy for Kurai, as they can confidently estimate each other’s actions. Antinov tended to take long trips to scout ahead of the newly named Azure squadron, providing intel and other useful services, while maintaining his nomadic nature and having his own adventures from time to time. Though eventually circumstance would again stand in his way.

Antinov’s main asset as a field operative was being small for his kind. While towering at 10’-12’, he was quite small for his age among Avalonians. This was mostly due to malnutrition, however, as a biologically immortal species, there is no age at which Avalonians stop growing. With a more stable, and in some cases excessive, diet, Anti shot up in size. Eventually he’d end up so large, that field work was difficult, if not impossible. From here, Antinov was forced to take a more command oriented role. Though he does take the occasional foray out, as his size makes him a terrifying asset in battle.

It's not uncommon to find the bird-like alien out and about on his own, even after his retirement from field work. Though one can never be sure if his allies are far behind or not, as sometimes an unlikely scout makes the best scout.

[hr]
During his life Antinov has shared time with many of the empire's more prominent members, from [user]Revika[/user] and her son [user]Casval[/user], to [user]Samira Voshkel[/user] and even the Seraphs [user]Aiko[/user], [user]Asaneth[/user] and [user]Niu Hibiki[/user]. In particular, with [user]Alice LeBlanc[/user]'s help, Antinov's body was upgraded to primarily synthetic components and technology equivalent to the Seraph class synthetics, affording him some of their abilities and further enhancing his ability to connect mentally to others. Antinov has been involved with [user]Savio[/user] and [user]Eidian[/user] as well, both as allies and adversaries. During his time with Kurai and the 1st Autonomous corps Antinov served under Savio on his ship.

Between his princely upbringing, strong connections and lineage, [user]Revika[/user] would deign him a worthy partner for her son [user]Casval[/user]. At first neither was thrilled by the prospect of an arranged marriage, but with time the two would fall for each-other on their own. It would seem both their mothers knew best, as the two have a lot in common. The two fiances formed quite a strong and intimate relationship, though a very much open relationship given their mutually amorous natures. This future puts Antinov directly in line for the Imperial throne some day, whenever Revika decides to abdicate to him and Casval. 

Antinov continues to suffer the consequences of his amorousness with the other princes of his homeland, making numerous bastards, and more legitimate offspring. Not that this isn't a bit of a common occurrence with Imperials. But between acting as a surrogate for [user]Aramis[/user] and [user]Anavale[/user], and a logistically tricky night with Niu, Antinov has become quite the sire. [user]Alva Hibiki[/user] as a father with Niu, and a sizeable clutch with his comrades Aramis and Anavale as a mother. The bird is still as shy as ever about his reproductive exploits, particularly on the maternal side.

[/collapse]
[collapse=⌘ Physiology & Anatomy:]
[b]P[/b]hysiologically, Antinov differs a bit from others of the Avalonian species. Antinov started out fairly typical, but in the many programs in which he was interned he was augmented and modified. The cusp of his enhancements and modifications came from [user]Alice LeBlanc[/user], who rebuilt Anti from the ground up to be much more like his mother [user]Kacylia[/user]. Though unlike his mother Antinov's modifications are well hidden, secret, this gives him an edge when people assume he's an average biological. Externally, you'd never know there's more to Antinov then any other of his species save for being in the larger size ranges.

Almost every part of his physiology directly mimics that of natural Avalonians, similar to his mother. Though on the surface inside and out the visuals are much closer to natural than the aesthetics of Kacylia's design. Internally, Antinov has most of the organs you'd expect of his species, though his lungs are greatly diminished in size due to their synthetic nature improving their functionality. Antinov possesses synthetic sub-dermal skin weaves and muscle augmentations to enhance durability and strength, alongside some extra synthetic organs similar to those found in Seraphs like [user]Niu Hibiki[/user], along with non-biological components.

Taking some of the space his lungs normally wound is a small combat Alcubierre drive, giving him abilities you'd see in Seraphs. Nestled just below his stomachs, in the coil of his small intestine, is a sizable reactor, fully integrated with his hybrid innards to feed the power needs of some of her body's functions. This reactor pumps out a significant amount of heat, giving Antinov a core body temperature ranging from 90°c at rest up to over 200°c while exerting himself (Which is much greater than the normal body temp of 60-70°c found in normal Avalonians). Being synthetic, Anti can handle those temperatures without any ill effects.

With all his enhancements, Antinov can actually survive in vacuum for extended periods, using reactor energy and specialized synthetic organs to recycle waste and keep him from asphyxiating. In the long run, it's overheating that would prove fatal to Antinov first if he were naked in space. Though with external cooling he would eventually asphyxiate after around two days. Unlike fully non-organic synths like Niu, Antinov has a limited amount of energy at his disposal. Generally this lasts from days to hours depending on exertion before needing to be re-fulled. However, the energy from his reactor is not needed for day to day survival in normal circumstances.

[hr]
[center][img=989973]AntiNude[/img]
[b]Antinov Nude.[/b][/center]
[hr]
[b]A[/b]ntinov's external anatomy is very typical of his species, actually discerning that he's not a natural Avalonian from outside is near impossible, save for some very minuscule features that are quite well hidden. That said, Anti is also considered quite exceptionally handsome for his species, with very idealized proportions and a mix of masculine and feminine features that are very desirable not just within his own species, but the larger Empire as well. Anti is a bit over average endowment for his age and sub-breed, and thanks to his parents, quite a spectacularly fertile specimen of an Avalonian.

Antinov is of course one of the larger of his species, especially considering he has not turned feral, most his size are feral, or at least partly feral, making Anti quite a rarity. Of course his enhancements do play a good part in not turning feral. These traits are of course shared with his virtually identical twin sister [user]Milena[/user].
[/collapse]
[collapse=⚔ Abilities & Equipment:]
[b]C[/b]ompared to the average member of his species, Antinov is much stronger and more durable than normal. And that's saying something, giving how tough his kind are overall. But in addition to more mundane augmentations, Antinov has some limited abilities granted by that combat Alcubierre drive. While they pale next to full Seraphs like [user]Niu Hibiki[/user], they are none the less impressive in their own right. Though mostly confined to offensive and defensive attacks of limited scale, versus any significant amount of spacial manipulation like locomotion via folding space, or forming an Einstein-Rosen bridge.

Antinov's abilities are greatly increased by external equipment. Notably external sources of power, as granted from his hard-suit or a suit of power-armour. Antinov is still young by Seraph standards as well, and only received the bulk of his upgrades later in life, making him but a novice at using the hallmark abilities of Seraphs. In this vane, Anti rarely employs them, aside from life or death scenarios.

[hr]
[b]A[/b]ntinov's non-integrated equipment is rather broad overall, encompassing whatever is needed for the task at hand. Though the Avalonian does have a preferred style. Generally Anti prefers pistols or sub-guns and medium and short bladed weapons. For the most-part Anti likes to fight in close, striking by surprise. But when charm and agility fail him in closing the distance, Antinov is no slouch with marksmanship. His adaptive nature and natural weapons mean that Antinov has a wide and rotating array of equipment, ranging from nothing more than a pretty dress and his own claws, up to advanced hard-suits and heavy weapons.

Given his unique body build and overall size, Anti tends to stick with a smaller array of clothing and protective gear, as getting things made to fit isn't cheap nor quick. From civilian clothes to combat armour, most objects need to be bespoke made for his anatomy and size. Or shipped great distances from Avalon where the rest of his kind mostly live. All that said, Anti does have a specific suit of combat armour he uses primarily. As with most of his equipment, it is more or less bespokely made by the special engineering division known as the BEO ([Bleu Eskadra Obrabotka] Blue Fleet Manufacturing).
[hr]
[collapse=🛡 Armour:]
[center][b][u]BEO. 'Protecta' PZB AV 5045-Avali[/u].[/b]
[img=989358]AntiArmour1[/img][/center]
[hr]
[b]T[/b]he Bleu Eskadra Obrabotka Protecta series PZB ([Promezhutochny Zashchita Boyet] Lit. Intermediate Combat Armour) AV ([Auf Vozrast] Version) 5045-Avali is what it says on the tin, so to speak (with some translation at least). Imperial naming schema for military equipment is very, very literal and plain. The idea being that the name is enough to tell anyone exactly what it is, versus flashy marketing names that only serve to add confusion much of the time. The Protecta series of composite hard-suit is a space-rated armour initially produced by the manufacturer Solius via their infantry armour solutions division. However, BEO also manufactures specialized versions under agreement from Solius. Hence the BEO Protecta tend to be more specialized, refined and improved over mass production models, while still retaining the same spirit. all in all the Protecta series is designed for high mobility without sacrificing protection, both from enemy aggressors and environment.

The initial armour is of itself a very high quality, but BEO takes it to a next level by refining and perfecting the crystalline structure of the component layers of the composites. Eliminating any microscopic imperfections for a much stronger product with chemical, heat and other more advanced processes. Ontop of refining the armour, onboard electronics are upgraded and re-tooled for the intended end user. In Antinov's case, this includes a re-kit for attachment points, inclusion of stealth technology (ranging from passive to active systems) and enhancement of the onboard optics to match and exceeds his own eyesight. Alongside modifications that let him use his wings freely, without having to remove any part of his armour or sacrificing protection for them.

[hr]
[center][b][u]Solius BMSK-7 Av PT[/u].[/b]
[img=989370]AntiBS1[/img][/center]
[hr]
[b]F[/b]orming the base layer of Antinov's armour is a standardized body-glove, using a system of attachment points and features standardized across all Imperial manufacturers long before Anti's time. The Solius BMSK-7 ([Boyet Modul'naya Sdavit Komplekt] Lit. Modular Compression Fighting Suit) is the latest revision, engineered to give the highest mobility, least weight, and greatest environmental protection possible. his standardized garment provides both compression, temperature control, waste management and other functions while serving as both a base layer for pressure suits and an undergarment for every day wear on-board space ships. Its tight fit leaves little to the imagination, but provides the wearer great support and comfort. Models made for aquatic and semi-aquatic species also have in-built moisture control. 

Av lists the garment as being fitted for an Avalonian, while PT is short for ([Perchatka-Telo] Body Glove) Most Imperial equipment is very self-explanatory in name, owing to the military's Russo origins. Ontop of this base layer a flexible ballistic layer combined with a heat dampening layer is worn. Ontop of this a third outer shell with mounting points is worn, that holds the hard outer layers of the suit in place. The bodysuit itself provides the airtight barrier, while the layers on top give rigidity and prevent the base layer from swelling due to pressure difference in vacuum, as was common in more early space suits.

[hr]
[center][img=989367]AntiArmour2[/img]
[b]From behind.[/b][/center]
[hr]
[b]A[/b] common flaw in armour for bipedal creatures, the hips and buttocks are left somewhat exposed, though for an Avalonian, the device that covers their tail-feathers serves to cover their buttocks without restricting movement in a noticeable way. Flexible and semi-ridged side skits can cover the hips, though Antinov does not use such, as they impede his movement significantly, and part of Anti's defences is being agile enough to keep enemies from getting a clean shot at weak points in his armour. Areas such as the tail-feather covers and wing covers are designed to open up when needed, but close air-tight. This allows the Avalonian user to use their wings without having to remove any part of their armour or sacrificing the ability to enter vacuum.

The wing coverings achieve this by using special elastic meta-materials that have a fantastic amount of stretch, and can pull tight with special electro-responsive contracting fibres built in, getting out of the way. Effectively, when the system is closed, the actual opening that needs to be sealed up is very small. Similar systems are in place for the clam-shell like tail-feather covers, though they need to be much less complex overall due to a more limited range of movement and far fewer joints.

[hr]
[center][img=989963]AntiArmour3[/img]
[b]wing covers in various states of opening.[/b][/center]
[hr]
[/collapse]

[collapse=≈ Voice:]
[b]A[/b]ntinov's voice is usually deep, smooth, powerful and quite rich. Most of the time Anti's voice is unmistakably masculine, though when the more feminine mood strikes his voice changes to match. Though even when feminine, Anti has quite a powerful and deep voice. As his avian appearance suggests though, Anti has a very wide range of vocalizations. In effect his species is able to mimic most any sound in their auditory range quite convincingly, from voices, to animal sounds and beyond.

Generally, Antinov has just a hint of Russian accent in his speech, not enough to mispronounce when speaking other languages outside Russo-Imperial, but it's readily apparent. Antinov is fluent in almost all the languages in the Empire and TSU thanks to deep cover immersion on both sides. Antinov is very confident and charismatic when he speaks, Owning to lots of practice speaking, bluffing and negotiating over his years as a spy. Antinov has a very lovely singing voice too, both in and out of his native language. 

[/collapse]

[collapse=ⓘ OOC Stuff.]
[b]I[/b]'m here to have fun, not for drama or arguments. My fun is also derived in part from pleasing my RP partner, there's a certain satisfaction to knowing someone else honestly enjoyed a scene. This is my main profile, not necessarily a hub, but Anti is the closest I'd get to a self representing character. It's best to direct any non-chat contact towards this profile.

I've heard from some I seem intimidating, I'm not an elitist, just a bit refined in knowing what I like and do not. The worst I'm going to do if you approach politely and in earnest is say no. I don't block people without reason, and don't like it done to me. As stated below, tell me you're not interested frankly, and I'll leave you be.

I also do not push anyone to do anything they do not like, I don't sneak my kinks into RP with someone who clearly has no interest. But mistakes can happen, please don't get upset if some secret no item comes up. Tell me and we can work around it, or just stop the scene. I'll do the same for you, though I tried to list everything that can ruin a scene in the interest of avoiding that issue in my profile.

And above all that, I try my best to be friendly, even though I'm a bit reclusive. It's much more likely you will get my attention from coming to me, than waiting for me to come to you. And know if I do come to you, most certainly something has managed to lure me out of my normal reclusiveness to approach.

Art credits can be found on my [url=https://www.furaffinity.net/user/kai/]FA page[/url] as linked above.

[u]Thank you for taking the time to read this[/u].
[/collapse]
[collapse=ⓘ If you wish to approach me.]
[b]I[/b]f you want to approach me in private, please do so only OOC. And [i]please[/i] don't just go with a simple 'hi.' Take some time to read over my profile, and approach with what you find interesting and what we would have in common.

You will be 100% more likely to catch my interest if I can see you've done more than look at my pictures, and glanced over 2-3 kinks you used to search me out.

I can respect that you don't want to waste time, but please try and address what you find interesting, what we have in common, what you might want to do that you think we will both enjoy. etc.

If you actually take the time and try to entice me with common interests you're 100% more likely to garner my attention and interest.~
[/collapse]
[collapse=ⓘ If I approach you.]
[b]C[/b]hances are I have fully read your profile, your kinks etc. I will do my best to be polite and greet you before getting into anything. If you started on your profile you want people to get down to business right away, I will. If it's not stated, and you want me to get to the point, just ask.

You can ignore things I usually require to get my interest that are in your No or Maybe list, it's 99.99% certain that I'm willing to leave those out if I'm approaching you and you dislike it. I'm not here to force others to do what they don't like. If it's an unlisted no, feel free to inform me, but I'll assume unlisted means no to slim maybe anyway.

Please do respond, even if it's to tell me you're entirely not interested. I'd rather be able to move on with my life than keep pining over someone who will never engage with me. I'm not going to hound you for saying no. But I won't just go away because I don't get an answer either.

The previous goes for notes too, take a moment and respond. I will always try and respond to notes other send me, even if it's to say "not interested", I'd like the same courtesy.

If you're not up for play right at the moment, or busy, tell me. I understand I'm not the only thing going on in the world. Don't assume I'm some terrible asshole because others are.

Don't lead me on. Don't set up a scene with me for later, with little to no intent to follow it through. Don't make plans to meet up another day, and never show up, or start ignoring me. If you plan to do that, just tell me you don't want to play straight up, I don't want to be lead around.
[/collapse]

Generally anti takes male pronouns, but he doesn't mind female ones. (Nor does his player, just please, no 'neutral' pronouns or herm pronouns, thanks. 18853024.name: Vore: Digestion, Realistic 1
18853024.description:
I'm hugely into realistic digestion. However, if I am frank, most I see who prefer "realistic digestion" really do not do a good job of capturing how the digestive process really works. Sure it's painful, at first, but the nerves will die and eventually leave you with nothing but irritation.

It's not just about pain, or bloodiness. Living creatures don't simply disintegrate from the extremities inwards. No no, it's far more gruesome and wonderful that that. When a living creature is immersed in acids (or bases) and enzymes, the digestive fluids will absorb slowly into the body, not just make it disintegrate from the surface in.

As the digestive cocktail works into the flesh, it will enter the bloodstream slowly and circulate within the body. Assuming the prey doesn't suffocate, this means their tissues will break down from within as well as from the outside. As time goes on, proteins denature, flesh acts similarly to cooking in a simmering pot.
